theme: Performance fixes and Nano Server fonts
highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 fixes a memory leak, improves managed-code performance, and adds FreeType-backed font support for Nano Server.
highlights_body: A CoTaskMem pixel-buffer leak in the SKImage raster failure path is resolved. GetEncodedText and SKRuntimeEffectUniforms see allocation and lookup optimisations. FreeType font support on Windows Nano Server containers closes a long-standing gap for that platform.

bullets.lead: Memory leak fixed in SKImage raster failure path
bullets.detail: When SKImage.Create failed to create a raster image, a CoTaskMem pixel buffer was not freed. The allocation is now correctly released on all failure paths.

bullets.lead: GetEncodedText avoids an intermediate string allocation
bullets.detail: The null-terminated fast path in GetEncodedText no longer allocates a temporary string, reducing per-call overhead on hot text rendering paths.

bullets.lead: SKRuntimeEffectUniforms lookup map pre-sized at construction
bullets.detail: The uniform name-to-index dictionary is now initialised with the known count, eliminating rehash allocations when effects define many uniforms.

bullets.lead: FreeType-backed font support for Windows Nano Server
bullets.detail: SkiaSharp now uses FreeType for font rendering on Windows Nano Server containers, where GDI is unavailable, resolving text rendering failures in that environment.

contributor_summaries.jeffska: Added FreeType font support for Windows Nano Server containers, fixing text rendering for GDI-less environments
